title: coffea coursiana subsp. littoralis
intro: Coffea coursiana subsp. littoralis, also known as littoral coffee, is a rare and lesser-known subspecies of the Coffea genus, native to Madagascar. It is rich in medicinal properties, including xanthines, caffeine, and other alkaloids, which have been used in traditional medicine to treat various ailments. The health benefits of littoral coffee include its potential to improve cognitive function, boost energy, and aid in weight loss, as well as its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. In herbal preparations, the seeds of Coffea coursiana subsp. littoralis are typically dried and then used as a tea or added to other herbal blends, although its use is not as widespread as that of other coffee species.
